web_results.description: ... Bannon has a one percent chance of becoming the GOP nominee, according to the betting site Polymarket, well behind the frontrunner, Vice President JD Vance, who has a 52 percent rating.

web_results.title: Scoop: Bannon quietly making moves toward 2028 run
web_results.description: Former Trump White House adviser Steve Bannon is laying the groundwork for a 2028 run for president, two people familiar with his thinking tell Axios. Why it matters: The MAGA godfather isn't serious about becoming president — that's not the point.

web_results.title: Steve Bannon Predicts Trump Will Run, and Win in 2028
web_results.description: A recent poll of CPAC attendees put Vice President J.D. Vance as the front-runner for the Republican nomination in 2028, but Steve Bannon had a much bolder prediction: a third term for Donald Trump.

web_results.title: 2028 United States presidential election - Wikipedia
web_results.description: Following a public clash with President Donald Trump in late 2025—particularly over demands to release Jeffrey Epstein files—online speculation surged about a potential 2028 presidential ticket with Marjorie Taylor Greene, dubbed "Massie-Greene 2028" by supporters. Massie has not declared any presidential candidacy and remains in Congress, where he faces a Trump-backed primary challenge in his 2026 reelection bid amid ongoing tensions, including his push for Epstein file releases. The following individuals have publicly denied interest in being a candidate: Greg Abbott, 48th governor of Texas (2015–present) Steve Bannon, former Senior Counselor to the President from Virginia (2017)
